utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . today i am seeing jose james . uh , date of birth 9/29/1957 . mr . james is a new patient who's having pain in his left shoulder and elbow . he fell five months ago going up a stairs , and the pain has been present since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi mr . james , i'm doctor isabella . how are you doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm okay . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's good . that's good . tell me what's brings you in today .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure . so i've been having constant pain in my left shoulder and left elbow for a while , about five months or so . hurts all the time . pretty bad at times .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm . that does not sound like fun . it sounds like you injured it going up the stairs ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , that's correct . it was icy , and i have to walk up a flight of stairs to my house . i stepped and my feet just went out . fell on my left side . seems like my shoulder and wrist took the brunt . it's been hurting since .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , that does seem like a likely cause of your symptoms . anything seem to make the pain worse ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i've always hit the gym and lifted weights . i've been trying to keep with my routine of two days a week , but it's been hard . the pain is worse when i lift , and i have n't been able to lift more than 15 or 20 pounds which is very frustrating .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . i can imagine it is when you're so accustomed to your routine .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: exactly . i really enjoy my exercise , but over the past one to two weeks , i've stopped hitting the gym because the pain just was n't worth it honestly . i do keep up with my walking , though . my wife and i walk about 30 minutes almost every day . we have done this since i retired from my office job last year .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do not blame you , mr . james . does anything seem to help the pain that you've tried ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not too much . i have iced a bit , but i have not taken anything for the pain . stopping exercise and some ice has helped a bit , but it still hurts enough for me to come to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , okay . how about any numbness or tingling ? have you felt that at any point ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , doc . just pain . no tingling or numbless , numbness , thankfully .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: understood . understood . all right . well , let's go ahead and take a look and see what's going on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . use my general physical exam template . mr . james , i'm going to gently press around your shoulder and elbow here to see where your pain might be stemming from . just let me know when it hurts .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . it hurts when you press there on my elbow and here on my shoulder .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . left shoulder and elbow , tender sa space , no warmth , erythema or deformity . positive hawkins-kennedy and neer's test . normal proximal and distar , distal upper extremity strength . intact median radial ulnar sensation and abduction to 90 degrees . normal empty tan , can test . okay , mr . james , w-what i think you are dealing with is impingement syndrome of your left shoulder . i do n't think there's an additional injury or issue with your wrist , but because everything is connected , you're experiencing pain in your wrist because of your shoulder . we do see this type of issue when someone has a fall , so it's good you came to see us , you came in to see us so we could help .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so what are the possible treatments ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , we have a few options you can try . first option would be to start with physical therapy . i would recommend two sessions per week as well as any other exercises they give you to do at home . we can start there and if that does n't improve your pain , then we could try a cortisone injection .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i like the idea of starting with the physical therapy and have a next step if it does n't help . i am not opposed to a shot but would like to try the therapy first .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . great . i'll get a referral order , and they will call you within the next day or two to get you scheduled .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: mm-hmm . also , please continue to ice , especially your shoulder , and rest as much as you can .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i will .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: give us a call or email us if you have symptoms worsen , if your symptoms worsen or do not improve with the therapy .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: will do . thank you . i appreciate your help .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . have a great day .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . assessment is impingement syndrome of left shoulder . plan discussed with patient . referral is requested for dr. martha rivera to be in physical therapy two days per week .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 64-year-old male presenting with left shoulder and elbow pain following a fall five months ago.
Left shoulder and elbow pain.
The patient reports constant pain in the left shoulder and elbow for five months.
The patient fell on his left side while walking up icy stairs five months ago, impacting his shoulder and wrist.
The doctor asks what factors exacerbate the patient's pain.
The patient reports pain is worse with lifting and he is unable to lift more than 15-20 pounds.
The patient stopped strength training 1-2 weeks ago but continues to walk 30 minutes daily and is a retired office worker.
The doctor asks what treatments the patient has tried for the pain.
The patient has used ice and stopped exercising, but denies taking any pain medication.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced any numbness or tingling.
The patient denies any numbness or tingling.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the shoulder and elbow.
The patient reports tenderness in the elbow and shoulder during palpation.

Physical exam findings include tender subacromial space, positive Hawkins-Kennedy and Neer's tests, normal strength, intact sensation, abduction to 90 degrees, and normal empty can test; the doctor assesses impingement syndrome.
The patient asks about possible treatment options.
The doctor recommends physical therapy twice weekly, with cortisone injection as a secondary option if pain persists.
The patient agrees to start with physical therapy.
The doctor will place a referral order for physical therapy.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue icing and resting.
The doctor instructs the patient to call or email if symptoms worsen or do not improve.

case_description: left shoulder and elbow pain; wrist pain
